blocks.html: What You'll Take Away

blocks.alt: A single calm figure looking toward a small set of simple goal markers, conveying focus and readiness to learn
blocks.html:
This short overview shows how I now build training in-house — faster, on-brand, and tracked. It also doubles as a tour: nearly every block on these slides is a different capability of the Course Builder. By the end you'll be able to:

  • Describe what the TeleTracking Course Builder is and the problem it solves
  • Explain how it turns ordinary content into polished, tracked courses
  • Recognize where people stay firmly in control
  • Identify what it takes to run it, and where it could grow
The Old Way vs The New Way
For years, building a single course meant licensed authoring tools, vendor lock-in, and long turnaround times. Here is the contrast in one view:

panels.callout: Slow, costly, and out of our hands.
panels.heading: The Old Way
items: Every edit reopens a proprietary tool
Each brand or language refresh means rework

panels.callout: Fast, and fully in our control.
panels.heading: The Course Builder
items: Edit plain text, rebuild in seconds
Re-brand or re-localize with no rebuild

blocks.html: What the Course Builder Is

infographic.footer: One in-house system. No licenses, no vendor wait.

left.callout: Slow, costly, and locked to a vendor.
left.heading: THE CHALLENGE
left.intro: Building courses the old way meant:
items: — every edit reopens a proprietary tool
— each brand or language redo starts over

cards.body: Our own system — no licenses, no vendor.
cards.title: In-house

cards.body: Registers completion in our learning platform.
cards.title: Tracked

infographic.subtitle: Plain content in — a branded, tracked course out.
infographic.title: What the Course Builder Is

blocks.html: It is not a replacement for our people's expertise — it is a power tool that lets a small team produce far more, far faster.
How It Works

blocks.alt: A simple left-to-right flow of three soft rounded shapes feeding into one polished output card, conveying an automated content-to-course pipeline

blocks.html: The system follows one clean path, every time:

entries.html: Author in Word or Markdown, or import an existing course.
entries.title: Bring content

entries.html: The engine structures it into one standard internal format.
entries.title: Convert

entries.html: TeleTracking colors, fonts, logo, and interactive blocks, applied automatically.
entries.title: Brand & render

entries.html: Out comes a standards-based course file, ready to upload and track.
entries.title: Package

blocks.html: One Build, Every Format
Because the content is stored once in a neutral format, the same course can be published to whatever standard our platform needs — with no rework.

OutputUse
SCORM 1.2Broadest LMS compatibility
SCORM 2004Richer tracking and sequencing
cmi5 / xAPIModern learning analytics
Author once. Publish to any standard. Re-brand or re-localize without rebuilding from scratch.
The Payoff, Illustrated
The engine can also render data directly into a course as a native, on-brand chart — generated from numbers, not pasted as a picture:

blocks.source: Illustrative example for this demo — not measured data.
blocks.title: Weeks to ship one course (illustrative)

blocks.html: These figures are illustrative, included to demonstrate the chart block. Real metrics would carry a cited source.
Where People Stay in Control

blocks.alt: A calm figure standing at a row of checkpoint markers along a path, hand raised in an approving gesture, conveying human review and sign-off at key gates
blocks.html: The system automates the assembly, not the judgment. People stay in the loop at every gate that matters — expand each one:

entries.html: Subject-matter experts write and approve the substance.
entries.title: Content & accuracy

entries.html: Every image is generated by a person, reviewed, and chosen on purpose.
entries.title: Course art

entries.html: A human confirms it looks right before it ships.
entries.title: Brand sign-off

entries.html: Nothing publishes without a person saying yes.
entries.title: Final approval

blocks.html: What It Takes to Run

blocks.alt: The Course Builder workspace in VS Code

blocks.html: The engine itself is lightweight and runs on an ordinary laptop — no special hardware. Here is the whole kit:

cards.teaser: Runs on a standard laptop — no servers, no GPUs.
cards.title: The engine

cards.teaser: Word, Markdown, or the browser-based template editor.
cards.title: Authoring

cards.teaser: An LLM to draft content and assist the build.
cards.title: The AI layer

cards.teaser: TeleTracking colors, fonts, and logo — already built in.
cards.title: Brand profile

blocks.html: Two Ways to Power It
The AI layer is a plug-in choice — the engine works the same either way. Flip each card:

entries.backHtml: The likely default — the business already has an enterprise plan, so there's nothing new to buy or host.
An on-premise model like Qwen or Mistral, for when content can't leave our walls. The engine doesn't care which it talks to.

blocks.html: Backend-agnostic by design — not locked to any one AI vendor.
The Principle Behind It
The machine speeds up the work. People still own what goes out the door.
Dig Deeper
Most learners do not need the internals — but when they do, a single button opens the detail in place, without cluttering the slide:

modal.heading: Under the hood
modal.html: The engine is a compact Python standard-library system — no servers, no GPUs. It reads Word or Markdown, converts to a neutral course format, applies a swappable brand profile, and emits SCORM 1.2, SCORM 2004, or cmi5. It runs air-gapped; the AI layer only assists authoring.

blocks.html: What It Could Grow Into

blocks.alt: A winding road with milestone markers, representing the capability roadmap ahead

blocks.html: A fuller capability roadmap accompanies this overview.
Proof: This Course Built Itself

You just experienced the product. This entire overview — the branding, the layout, the comparison, the infographic, the process steps, the chart, the accordion, the sorting check, the flashcards, the knowledge check — was authored as plain text and assembled by the Course Builder. What you see is what it produces.

blocks.alt: This very course, built and rendered by the Course Builder

blocks.html: Check Your Understanding

blocks.feedback: Exactly. It is our own system, it automates the assembly, and people own the content, the art, and the final sign-off.
blocks.feedbackIncorrect: Not quite. It is in-house (not a purchased license), and people stay in control of content, art, and approval — the system speeds up the assembly, it does not replace judgment.

options.html: A new licensed authoring tool we purchased from a vendor
An in-house system that turns content into branded, tracked courses, with people in control at every gate
A fully automated AI that writes and publishes training with no human involvement
A replacement for our instructional design team

blocks.prompt: What is the best one-line description of the TeleTracking Course Builder?

blocks.html: Where It Stands, and Your Part
The Course Builder is real, working, and already producing branded courses that track in our platform. I built it, and I want to keep developing it — growing what it can do for TeleTracking.
Approve this for more development by me.
A nod that this is worth pursuing is all it takes. I'll keep building.
